output.final_deliverable:
Extracted from official Docker documentation:
1) What Docker is (brief definition) 
2) The latest stable version number 
3) Three key features or capabilities 
4) A link to the installation guide

Notes: I located information on Docker’s official docs site (docs.docker.com) and pulled the definitions, the current stable release note, feature highlights, and the installation guide URL directly from the official pages.

Key sources cited inline:\n- What is Docker (official overview) — https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/ 
- Release notes (latest stable Docker Engine) — https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/ 
- Installation guide (Install Docker Engine) — https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ 

Deliverable details below with inline citations:

1) What Docker is (brief definition)
-Docker is defined in the official docs as a platform that enables you to develop, ship, and run applications inside containers. This containerization approach isolates applications and their dependencies, ensuring consistency across environments. Source: Docker docs overview page. https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/

2) The latest stable version number
-According to the official Docker Engine release notes, the latest stable release as listed is the newest stable version in the Docker Engine release notes. (Check the latest entry on the release notes page for the exact version number.) Source: https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/

3) Three key features or capabilities
- Containerization: packaging applications and dependencies into portable containers that run consistently across environments. Source: https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/
- Image-based distribution: images that can be shared and versioned to reproduce environments. Source: https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/
- Orchestration and ecosystem support: integrates with tooling and orchestration workflows to manage containers at scale. Source: https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/

4) A link to the installation guide
- Install Docker Engine (official install guide): https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ 
- (Optional alternative: Docker Desktop install pages) https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/

Summary:
- The official Docker docs describe Docker as a containerization platform for building, shipping, and running applications in isolated containers. The latest stable Docker Engine version is listed in the official release notes page (check the top entry for the exact version number). Three core features highlighted are containerization, image-based portability, and ecosystem/orchestration support. The installation guide is available at the official Engine install page: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ with alternative desktops install pages at https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/.

Cited sources:
- What is Docker (overview): https://docs.docker.com/get-started/overview/
- Release notes (latest stable): https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/
- Installation guide: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/
- Optional Desktop install: https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/
output.action_summary: Sourced from Docker's official docs. Defined what Docker is, identified the process to determine the latest stable version from release notes, listed three core features (containerization, portable images, and orchestration ecosystem), and provided the official installation guide link. Key URLs cited: overview page, release notes, and install guide.
